tldr: Replace decorative Lottie animations with static Phosphor icons (six weights, 1200+ icons) where motion isn't essential — primarily a scope reduction, not a swap.
why_switch: Static SVG icons load instantly and ship no runtime player
Phosphor's six weight variants cover most decorative uses Lottie filled
MIT licensed and free across web, React, Vue, and Flutter
Removes dependency on the LottieFiles platform/subscription
Easier to keep accessible and consistent than animated assets
what_to_export: Inventory of every Lottie animation and where it renders
Classification: decorative, status, loading, full illustration
Source .json files plus After Effects originals
Brand colors and sizes applied to each animation
List of animations to keep as motion vs replace with static
Mapping spreadsheet from each Lottie to a Phosphor candidate
export_method: Walk every Lottie surface and decide whether motion is meaningful or decorative. For decorative ones, pick a Phosphor icon and weight that conveys the same idea. Export the .json sources from your LottieFiles workspace via the official UI for archival. Keep before/after screenshots for design review.
import_method: Install the Phosphor package for your stack (e.g. @phosphor-icons/react, @phosphor-icons/vue, phosphor_flutter) per its official docs and import only the icons you actually use. Replace each chosen Lottie surface with the corresponding Phosphor component, set weight and color, and remove the Lottie JSON + player dependency once nothing references them. Keep Lottie isolated to surfaces where motion is required.

concept_mapping.note: Pair with a CSS keyframe rotation.
Add a brief CSS transition if motion is desired.
Use illustrations or keep Lottie for hero scenes.
Re-implement motion via CSS where needed.
Consult Phosphor docs for current Figma assets.

gotchas: Phosphor is icons, not animations — only swap where motion isn't essential
Heavy illustrations and brand motion will still need Lottie or another tool
Importing many weights of the same icon inflates bundle size — pick a default
Removing the Lottie player requires verifying no leftover imports remain
Get design sign-off per swap; static icons can read very differently
Some Lottie concepts (mascots, full scenes) have no icon equivalent at all

email.subject: Replacing decorative Lottie animations with Phosphor icons
email.body:
Hey team,

I'd like to trim our LottieFiles surface area by replacing decorative animations with static Phosphor icons where motion isn't meaningful. Phosphor gives us six weight variants and 1200+ icons under MIT, which covers most of our 'icon-shaped Lottie' cases without the player overhead.

Approach: inventory every Lottie usage, mark each as keep/replace, then map the replace bucket to Phosphor components. Anything load-bearing in motion (loaders, brand scenes) stays on Lottie. Estimated effort: 4-12 hours.

Let me know which animations you consider non-negotiable before I start.

Thanks
